mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeclipper on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeClipper is a lightweight web service that provides a user-friendly web console, APIs, and CLI tools for managing Kubernetes clusters. Installing KubeClipper allows you to efficiently deploy and manage Kubernetes clusters across various environments.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for KubeClipper
steps.description: First, create a dedicated namespace for KubeClipper to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Deploy KubeClipper Components
steps.description: Apply the KubeClipper manifests to deploy the necessary components.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check if the KubeClipper pods are running successfully.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KubeClipper will show the KubeClipper pods running in the 'kubeclipper' namespace. You can access the web console by navigating to http://localhost:8080 in your web browser.

uninstall.title: Remove KubeClipper
uninstall.description: To uninstall KubeClipper, delete the namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeClipper
upgrade.description: To upgrade KubeClipper, apply the new version of the manifests. Make sure to check for breaking changes in the release notes.

troubleshooting.title: Pods Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: If the KubeClipper pods are not starting, check the events in the namespace for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description: If you cannot access the KubeClipper web console, ensure that the service is correctly configured and check the service endpoints.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If pods are in a pending state, verify that your cluster has sufficient resources (CPU, memory) available.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
